rationale.https://doi.org/10.65215/2q58a426: "Attention Is All You Need" (Vaswani et al., 6546 citations) — THE original transformer paper. Primary source for query; no top-10 is complete without it.
rationale.https://jalammar.github.io/illustrated-t: Jay Alammar's Illustrated Transformer — the most widely-referenced visual explanation of transformer architecture and attention. Canonical secondary source.
rationale.https://lilianweng.github.io/posts/2018-: Lilian Weng's 'Attention? Attention!' — seminal, comprehensive blog post covering all major attention variants. One of the most-cited blog references in ML.
rationale.https://d2l.ai/chapter_attention-mechani: Dive into Deep Learning textbook chapter on attention mechanisms and transformers — authoritative textbook, widely used in courses. Covers both theory and implementation.
rationale.https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers: HuggingFace attention documentation — practitioner-facing doc on attention variants (sparse, LSH, local). Essential for developers using the dominant transformer library.
rationale.https://arxiv.org/html/2604.00965v1: 'Understanding Transformers and Attention Mechanisms' — tutorial paper specifically about the attention mechanism in modern LMs. Recent (2026) and targeted.
rationale.https://arxiv.org/html/2302.07730v4: 'Transformer models: an introduction and catalog' — comprehensive survey of transformer variants. Useful reference for scope of the field.
rationale.https://doi.org/10.31219/osf.io/m6gcn: 'Attention Mechanism, Transformers, BERT, and GPT: Tutorial and Survey' — explicit tutorial+survey covering seq2seq attention, self-attention, multihead, BERT, GPT. 80 citations.
rationale.https://www.baeldung.com/cs/attention-me: Baeldung developer-oriented article on attention in transformers — concise, practical, widely read by practitioners.
rationale.https://web.stanford.edu/~jurafsky/slp3/: Stanford Jurafsky/Martin transformer slides — authoritative academic slides from the SLP3 textbook authors. Covers self-attention mechanism directly.

notes: Pool of 44 contains strong signal: the original paper, D2L textbook, top blogs (Alammar, Weng), HuggingFace docs, and Stanford slides. Also contains significant noise: Open Library false positives (Husserl, Rosmini, World's Fair, Zacks — same 4 entries as the books pool), domain-application papers (bearing fault diagnosis, satellite detection, drug-drug interaction, ASR), and off-topic entries (GPT-3 Creative Fiction, tokenization article, Mamba, MatMul-free). Excluded domain papers despite high citation counts (Informer 5886×, time-series bearing 485×) because they use transformer attention for specific applications rather than explaining the mechanism.
